---
title: "Post-Encoder Modules"
---

## Overview

Post-encoder modules are optional **torch-to-torch** processing stages applied
**after** `ModelBase::forward()` and **before** channel decoders. They transform
a model's raw output tensor (typically `[B, C, H, W]`) into a form suitable for
decoding — most commonly collapsing spatial dimensions to `[B, C]`.

Post-encoder modules are **model-agnostic**. They are not owned by
`GeneralEncoderModel` or any other model subclass. `SlotAssembler` holds the
active module as session configuration and applies it to the **first output slot**
of whatever model is loaded — encoders, segmentation models, or JSON-defined
`RuntimeModel` instances.

```
ModelBase::forward()  →  raw output [B, C, H, W]
        │
        ▼
PostEncoderModule::apply()   (optional — SlotAssembler session config)
        │
        ▼
ChannelDecoder::decode()     (e.g. TensorToFeatureVector, TensorToMask2D)
        │
        ▼
DataManager
```

**Source:** `src/DeepLearning/post_encoder/`

## Abstract interface

```cpp
// src/DeepLearning/post_encoder/PostEncoderModule.hpp

class PostEncoderModule {
public:
    virtual ~PostEncoderModule() = default;

    [[nodiscard]] virtual std::string name() const = 0;

  /// @pre features must be defined
    [[nodiscard]] virtual at::Tensor apply(at::Tensor const & features) const = 0;

  /// @param input_shape Feature shape excluding batch dim, e.g. {384, 7, 7}
    [[nodiscard]] virtual std::vector<int64_t>
    outputShape(std::vector<int64_t> const & input_shape) const = 0;
};
```

The header forward-declares `at::Tensor` to remain torch-free at inclusion time.
Concrete `.cpp` files include libtorch directly.

Modules chain via `PostEncoderPipeline` (itself a `PostEncoderModule`). An empty
pipeline is a pass-through.

## Implementations

### GlobalAvgPoolModule

`[B, C, H, W] → [B, C]` via `at::adaptive_avg_pool2d({1, 1})` with spatial
squeeze.

Registry key: `"global_avg_pool"`. Sets `collapses_spatial_dims = true`.

`apply()` validates 4D input with `B, C, H, W >= 1`. Invalid input throws
`std::invalid_argument` after `spdlog::debug` logging.

### SpatialPointExtractModule

Extracts the C-dimensional feature vector at a 2-D point in source-image
coordinates. Registry key: `"spatial_point"`. Also collapses spatial dims.

| Mode | Mechanics |
|---|---|
| `Nearest` | Scale point to feature-map pixel, clamp, slice `[B, C, iy, ix]` |
| `Bilinear` | Normalise to `[-1, 1]`, `grid_sample` with `align_corners=true`, border padding |

The module is **stateful**: call `setPoint(Point2D<float>)` before each
`apply()`. `SlotAssembler::_updateSpatialPoint()` reads the first point from
`SpatialPointModuleParams::point_key` at the current frame.

Bilinear mode requires `H >= 2` and `W >= 2` (normalisation divides by
`H - 1`, `W - 1`). Constructor requires positive `source_image_size`.

`SpatialPointExtractModule` forces single-frame batch inference
(`requiresSingleFrameBatch()` returns true) because the query point is
frame-specific.

### PostEncoderPipeline

Ordered chain of modules. `apply()` threads the tensor through each stage.
`outputShape()` propagates shape through all stages.

## Registry

`PostEncoderModuleRegistry` is a singleton mapping keys to metadata, parameter
schemas, and factories.

| Method | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `moduleKeys()` | Sorted registered keys |
| `getMetadata(key)` | Display name, description, `collapses_spatial_dims` |
| `getSchema(key)` | `ParameterSchema` for `AutoParamWidget` |
| `create(key, params_json, source_image_size)` | Instantiate module |
| `collapsesSpatialDims(key)` | Whether module maps `[B,C,H,W]` → `[B,C]` |
| `outputShape(key, input_shape, ...)` | Shape propagation without inference |

`create()` returns `nullptr` for `"none"`, empty key, or unknown keys.

| Key | Module | `collapses_spatial_dims` |
|---|---|---|
| `"global_avg_pool"` | `GlobalAvgPoolModule` | true |
| `"spatial_point"` | `SpatialPointExtractModule` | true |

Modules self-register at static init via `RegisterPostEncoderModule<Params>` in
their `.cpp` file. Parameter structs live in `PostEncoderModuleParams.hpp`:

```cpp
struct GlobalAvgPoolModuleParams {};

struct SpatialPointModuleParams {
    InterpolationMode interpolation = InterpolationMode::Nearest;
    std::string point_key;
};
```

To add a new module:

1. Implement `PostEncoderModule` with `outputShape()`.
2. Define an `rfl` params struct (+ `ParameterUIHints` in `PostEncoderParamSchemas.hpp`).
3. Add `RegisterPostEncoderModule<Params>(...)` in the module `.cpp`.

## Model recommendations

Models may suggest a default post-encoder via
`ModelBase::recommendedPostEncoderModule()`:

| Model | Recommended key |
|---|---|
| `GeneralEncoderModel` | `"global_avg_pool"` |
| `RuntimeModel` | `RuntimeModelSpec::recommended_post_encoder` (optional JSON field) |
| Other models | `""` (none) |

`ModelInfo::recommended_post_encoder` aggregates this for the widget. On model
selection, `DeepLearningPropertiesWidget` pre-selects the recommended module in
`DeepLearningState::post_encoder_params`. The user can override via
`PostEncoderWidget` for any loaded model.

## Integration with SlotAssembler

Post-encoder is owned by `SlotAssembler::Impl::post_encoder_module`, configured
through:

```cpp
void configurePostEncoderModule(
    dl::PostEncoderStepDescriptor const & params,
    ImageSize source_image_size = {});
```

`PostEncoderStepDescriptor` (`SlotBindingTypes.hpp`):

```cpp
struct PostEncoderStepDescriptor {
    std::string module_key = "none";   // "none" or empty → no module
    std::string parameters_json = "{}";
};
```

### Per-frame pipeline

Every inference mode follows the same pattern:

```
_updateSpatialPoint()          // spatial_point only
    → ModelBase::forward()
    → applyPostEncoderToFirstOutputSlot()   // first output slot only
    → decodeOutputs()
```

Free functions in `PostEncoderOutputTransform.hpp`:

- `applyPostEncoderToFirstOutputSlot()` — transform first output tensor
- `effectiveOutputSlots()` — propagate shapes for decoder UI and validation

`ModelBase::outputSlots()` always reports raw model shapes.
`SlotAssembler::currentModelInfo()` uses `effectiveOutputSlots()` so output
binding widgets reflect post-encoder shape changes.

### Lifecycle

- `loadModel()` and `resetModel()` clear `post_encoder_module`
- `PostEncoderWidget::syncToAssembler()` re-applies saved params after weights
  load (`_loadModelIfReady()`)
- Parameter changes call `configurePostEncoderModule()` immediately when a model
  is loaded (weights not required for configuration)

**Targeting:** post-encoder applies to the **first** model output slot only.
Per-slot targeting is planned.

**Recurrent inference:** recurrent tensor bindings cache the **post-encoder
output** tensor (the same tensor passed to decoders) for the next frame's input
injection.

### Decoder consistency

`dl::constraints::validDecodersForPostEncoder()` queries
`collapsesSpatialDims()`:

| Post-encoder | Allowed decoders |
|---|---|
| `"none"` or spatial-preserving | Mask, Point, Line, FeatureVector |
| Spatial-collapsing (`global_avg_pool`, `spatial_point`) | `FeatureVectorDecoderParams` only |

`DeepLearningPropertiesWidget::_enforcePostEncoderDecoderConsistency()` updates
output slot decoder combos when the post-encoder module changes.

See [Session Constraints](constraints.qmd).

## Widget UI

The **Post-Encoder Module** section is rendered by `PostEncoderWidget` in the
dynamic slot panels — placed after memory inputs and **before** outputs (matching
the inference pipeline order).

| Component | Role |
|---|---|
| Module combo | `PostEncoderModuleRegistry::moduleKeys()` + `(None)` |
| Description label | `getMetadata(key).description` |
| Parameter panel | `AutoParamWidget` from `getSchema(key)` |
| `point_key` combo | Dynamic DataManager combo (`ParameterUIHints`) |

### Data flow

On construction:

1. Populate module combo from registry
2. Restore from `DeepLearningState::postEncoderParams()`
3. Rebuild parameter panel for selected key

On parameter change:

1. Read `{module_key, parameters_json}` from combo + `AutoParamWidget::toJson()`
2. Update `DeepLearningState::setPostEncoderParams()` (workspace JSON)
3. Call `configurePostEncoderModule()` when model is loaded
4. Enforce decoder consistency on output slot widgets

See [PostEncoderWidget](../ui/DeepLearning_Widget/UI/Helpers/helpers.qmd#postencoderwidget)
and [Schema-Driven Forms](../ui/schema_driven_forms.qmd).

## Batch inference and TensorData output

For batch runs with `TensorToFeatureVector`, `ResultProcessor` collects
`(frame_index, std::vector<float>)` pairs and merges them into a single
`TensorData::createOrdinal2D` matrix at batch completion. Each row is one
frame; each column is one feature channel.

## Future work

- **Per-output-slot targeting** — bind post-encoder to a named output slot on
  multi-output models
- **`prepareFrame(DataManager&, int)` on modules** — move spatial-point lookup
  into the module interface
- **Decoder-roundtrip recurrent feedback** — decode → re-encode for models that
  feed decoded results back as inputs

## Testing

| Test file | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `tests/DeepLearning/post_encoder/PostEncoderModules.test.cpp` | Modules, pipeline, registry |
| `tests/DeepLearning/post_encoder/PostEncoderOutputTransform.test.cpp` | `applyPostEncoderToFirstOutputSlot`, `effectiveOutputSlots` |

```bash
ctest --preset linux-clang-release -R "post_encoder"
```

## Related documentation

- [General Encoder Model](general_encoder.qmd) — backbone that recommends `global_avg_pool`
- [ModelInfo](models_v2/ModelInfo.qmd) — `recommended_post_encoder` and effective outputs
- [Session Constraints](constraints.qmd) — decoder alternatives per module
- [TensorToFeatureVector](channel_decoding/TensorToFeatureVector.qmd) — typical decoder after spatial collapse
